metadata.notes: All five positions verified as of April 29, 2026. Email addresses are confirmed institutional patterns; final spot-verification (2-5 minutes per contact) is required immediately before Block 6 send. Record verified email in the 'verified_email' field before proceeding.

position.title: Co-Editor-in-Chief, Just Security; Anne and Joel Ehrenkranz Professor of Law
position.verification_source: justsecurity.org masthead; NYU Law faculty page

contact.email_pattern_note: NYU format: first.last@nyu.edu — confirmed institutional pattern
contact.email_alternate_note: Just Security editorial contact; try if NYU address bounces

personalization.domain_lead_rationale: Just Security's editorial focus is national security law, rule of law, and executive accountability — Domains 28, 29, and 6 are exact editorial beats. Just Security published FISA 702 analysis (Domain 25 hook), war powers analysis (Domain 28), and prosecutorial accountability pieces (Domain 29) in April 2026.
personalization.key_hook: Domain 28 OLC memo constitutional analysis: internal contradiction of applying two-part war powers test to 'law enforcement' characterization; forward-reach implication for DEA indictments in Iran, North Korea, Lebanon, Russia
personalization.secondary_hook: Domain 29 SPLC indictment (April 21) legal defect analysis; April 25 state bar interference rulemaking (McDade-Murtha)
personalization.april_2026_topical_hook: Just Security's FISA 'Fool's Gold' analysis (cited in Domain 25) makes surveillance domain directly relevant; SPLC indictment (April 21) is April 2026 Domain 29 core case
personalization.ask_type: Feedback on OLC memo analysis accuracy + consideration of Domain 28 or Domain 29 as Just Security forum submission
personalization.placeholder_to_fill: {{RECENT_JUST_SECURITY_ARTICLE}} — visit justsecurity.org/author/ryan-goodman/ for most recent piece

subject_lines.option_a: Domain 28 / Operation Absolute Resolve — OLC memo analysis + statutory gap — Just Security forum piece?
subject_lines.option_b: Independent research: prosecutorial weaponization (Domain 29, SPLC indictment) + war powers — seeking Just Security feedback
subject_lines.option_c: Framework + FISA 702 analysis: judicial independence and surveillance accountability — for Just Security review
subject_lines.recommendation_note: Option A is the most specific and curiosity-generating; it names the operation and the specific analytical contribution (statutory gap). Option B works if Goodman's most recent work is on prosecutorial accountability rather than war powers.

path_specific_additions.path_a: The complete 35-domain framework is production-ready and available now.
path_specific_additions.path_a_37: The 35-domain framework is in Phase 1 distribution; Domain 37 (Federal Executive Interference, 2026 Midterms) is available as a separately targeted document for election-protection contacts.
path_specific_additions.path_b: The framework is in active development — I'm soliciting practitioner feedback at this stage, before wider distribution. Your read on what the analysis gets wrong is particularly valuable right now.

contact.email_pattern_note: Brennan Center format: first-initial + last@brennancenter.org — confirmed
contact.email_alternate_note: No confirmed alternate; if primary bounces, route through brennancenter.org contact form

personalization.domain_lead_rationale: Brennan Center is the primary institutional tracker of voting rights litigation and state-level suppression — Domains 1 and 33 draw on Brennan Center data extensively. Weiser leads the Democracy Program directly.
personalization.key_hook: Four-state SAVE Act analog enactment wave (FL, MS, SD, UT, April 2026) as evidence of coordinated state-level fallback after Senate defeat; coalition fracture analysis (Collins, Murkowski, Tillis, McConnell bloc) as institutionalist target
personalization.secondary_hook: Domain 1 FISA/election security collision; Arizona ballot initiative (621K signatures); Domain 33 ballot initiative suppression wave (295 bills in 43 states, 2025)
personalization.april_2026_topical_hook: Brennan Center actively publishing FISA 702 and voting rights coverage — Domains 1 and 25 directly current. Four-state SAVE Act wave is the most recent state-level development in Domain 1.
personalization.ask_type: Scholarly feedback — two specific questions about the coordination framing and institutionalist bloc durability
personalization.placeholder_to_fill: {{RECENT_BRENNAN_CENTER_PUBLICATION}} — check brennancenter.org/our-work for most recent Weiser piece or Brennan Center voting rights report

subject_lines.option_a: Framework + April 2026 voting rights crisis: four-state SAVE Act wave + ballot initiative suppression — Brennan Center feedback
subject_lines.option_b: Independent research on voting rights and federalism — builds on Brennan Center work — seeking feedback
subject_lines.option_c: 35-domain democratic reform framework — Domain 1 (electoral reform) — academic review requested
subject_lines.recommendation_note: Option A is the most topical for April 2026 — directly references events Weiser is currently tracking. Option B is safer but less specific.

path_specific_additions.path_a: The complete 35-domain framework is production-ready and available now.
path_specific_additions.path_a_37: Domain 37 (Federal Executive Interference, 2026 Midterms) is being distributed separately to election-protection contacts in Week 2–3, given its targeted institutional relevance to Democracy Docket, Campaign Legal Center, and state AGs in competitive states.
path_specific_additions.path_b: The framework is in active development — practitioner feedback at this stage directly shapes Phase 2 expansion priorities.

position.title: Frank Stanton Professor of the Practice of Public Leadership; Director, Nonviolent Action Lab

contact.email_pattern_note: HKS faculty format: first-initial + last@hks.harvard.edu — confirmed
contact.email_alternate_note: No confirmed alternate; if primary bounces, try echenoweth@harvard.edu

personalization.domain_lead_rationale: The framework's theory of change section is built directly on Chenoweth's 3.5% threshold finding and the 'End of People Power' complicating argument. Her institutional context (Harvard funding freeze, Domain 27) adds personal relevance.
personalization.key_hook: 3.5% threshold as organizing metric — 7M No Kings participants vs. 11.6M U.S. threshold; application of Poland/South Africa comparative methodology to current U.S. trajectory; academic question about whether U.S. is closer to Poland or Hungary on reversibility spectrum
personalization.secondary_hook: Harvard $2.2B funding freeze documented in Domain 27 — her own institution is in the framework's evidentiary record
personalization.april_2026_topical_hook: Chenoweth's 'Why Gen-Z Is Rising' (Journal of Democracy, January 2026) and in-progress 'End of People Power' are both pre-filled in template. Harvard funding freeze (April 2026) is the most recent topical hook.
personalization.most_recent_work_default: Why Gen-Z Is Rising, Journal of Democracy, January 2026
personalization.ask_type: Scholarly feedback — two specific questions about the Poland/Hungary reversibility assessment and the organizational depth comparison
personalization.placeholder_to_fill: {{RECENT_CHENOWETH_WORK}} — default is pre-filled; verify it is still the most recent at hks.harvard.edu/faculty/erica-chenoweth

subject_lines.option_a: Research builds on your 3.5% threshold — seeking correction on nonviolent action application to current U.S. context
subject_lines.option_b: 35-domain democratic renewal framework — theory of change grounded in Chenoweth nonviolent action research — academic feedback
subject_lines.option_c: Harvard Kennedy School research collaboration: democratic renewal diagnostic & mobilization strategy
subject_lines.recommendation_note: Option A is the strongest — it leads with the specific threshold finding and frames the ask as a correction request rather than an endorsement request. Option B is acceptable but less specific.

path_specific_additions.path_a: The complete 35-domain framework is production-ready and available now.
path_specific_additions.path_a_37: The 35-domain framework is in Phase 1 distribution; Domain 37 (Federal Executive Interference, 2026 Midterms) will be distributed to election-protection organizations in a targeted Phase 2.
path_specific_additions.path_b: The framework is at an active development stage — academic feedback before wider distribution is exactly what shapes the analysis's defensibility in institutional contexts.

contact.email_pattern_note: Protect Democracy format: first@protectdemocracy.org — confirmed for executive directors
contact.email_alternate_note: If primary bounces, use Protect Democracy contact form at protectdemocracy.org/contact

personalization.domain_lead_rationale: Protect Democracy is co-plaintiff or amicus in multiple active cases documented in Domains 6 and 29. Bassin's litigation posture spans judicial independence and retaliatory prosecution — both are framework core domains. Protect Democracy is also the Common Cause plaintiff in the national voter database lawsuit (Domain 37 hook for Path A+37).
personalization.key_hook: Domain 6 enforcement gap analysis (the three-track response is structurally insufficient when DOJ institutional capture prevents federal criminal referral) — genuine question about whether Protect Democracy's active docket addresses this gap through creative standing or equitable enforcement theories
personalization.secondary_hook: Domain 29 SPLC indictment amicus work; SPLC's April 28 defense motions (Judge Emily Marks, N.D. Ala.) — most recent Domain 29 development
personalization.april_2026_topical_hook: Protect Democracy's active co-plaintiff status in multiple Domain 6 and Domain 29 cases; Common Cause voter database lawsuit (Domain 37) for Path A+37
personalization.ask_type: Meeting request (20 minutes) — most valuable contact type for implementation strategy feedback
personalization.placeholder_to_fill: {{RECENT_PROTECT_DEMOCRACY_FILING}} — check protectdemocracy.org/work for most recent case or publication (case name, court, filing date). Also fill {{YOUR_AVAILABILITY}} with 2–3 specific time windows.

subject_lines.option_a: Framework + prosecutorial weaponization: domain-29 SPLC analysis and domain-06 judicial threat documentation — Protect Democracy input
subject_lines.option_b: Litigation & implementation strategy: democratic renewal framework (judicial independence + constitutional reform) — brief call?
subject_lines.option_c: Independent research on DOJ capture and judicial independence — builds on Protect Democracy litigation — seeking 20 minutes
subject_lines.recommendation_note: Option B is cleanest for a meeting-request template — specific domains named, clear ask in subject line. Option A works if Bassin's most recent work is on prosecutorial accountability specifically.

path_specific_additions.path_a: The complete 35-domain framework is production-ready and available now.
path_specific_additions.path_a_37: Domain 37 (Federal Executive Interference, 2026 Midterms) includes a section specifically on Protect Democracy's Common Cause national voter database lawsuit and the post-CASA vacatur pathway — I'm distributing that document separately to election-protection contacts in Week 2.
path_specific_additions.path_b: The framework is in active development — practitioner input on the enforcement gap and litigation strategy shapes the implementation pathways section directly.

position.title: Firm Chair, Elias Law Group; Founder, Democracy Docket
position.verified_date: 2026-04-29 (updated 2026-05-15: left Perkins Coie 2021, confirmed at elias.law)

contact.email_pattern_note: Elias Law Group format: confirmed primary contact at elias.law; Perkins Coie address is stale (left 2021)
contact.email_alternate_note: Democracy Docket press contact; try if primary bounces
contact.social_x_note: @marceelias is active on X — confirmed posting in April 2026

domain_lead: Domain 1 — Voting Rights and Elections (litigation tracker)
personalization.domain_lead_rationale: Democracy Docket is the primary institutional source for active voting rights litigation — it is cited extensively in Domain 1 and in the Litigation Tracker 2026. Elias's June–July 2026 decision timeline (Watson v. RNC, Louisiana v. Callais) creates immediate topical relevance. Democracy Docket is cited 12+ times in Domain 37 as the primary NVRA enforcement vehicle — especially high-value for Path A+37.
personalization.key_hook: Watson v. RNC (mail ballot deadline) and Louisiana v. Callais (VRA Section 2) — both expected June–July 2026; Domain 1 case strategy analysis; Litigation Tracker 2026 as working tool for Democracy Docket's documentation
personalization.secondary_hook: NVRA 90-day quiet period (August 7, 2026) pre-filing strategy — cited in P.S. of email template; this is the highest-ROI Domain 37 hook for Elias
personalization.april_2026_topical_hook: Four-state SAVE Act analog enactment wave (FL, MS, SD, UT) is Domain 1 most recent development. Watson v. RNC and Louisiana v. Callais are active Democracy Docket tracked cases.
personalization.ask_type: Feedback request + co-publication potential (litigation tracker additions)
personalization.placeholder_to_fill: Verify current status of Watson v. RNC and Louisiana v. Callais at democracydocket.com — update Domain 1 case status references in template if anything has changed since last check

subject_lines.option_a: Democracy Docket collaboration: electoral reform framework + litigation tracker alignment
subject_lines.option_b: Framework + Watson v. RNC / Louisiana v. Callais analysis — domain 1 litigation tracker — Democracy Docket feedback
subject_lines.option_c: Independent research on electoral reform and voter suppression — builds on Democracy Docket tracking — seeking input
subject_lines.recommendation_note: Option B is strongest — names the specific active cases that Elias is tracking, signaling the sender has read Democracy Docket's work rather than sent a generic outreach email.

path_specific_additions.path_a: The complete 35-domain framework is production-ready and available now.
path_specific_additions.path_a_37: Domain 37 (Federal Executive Interference, 2026 Midterms) is being distributed to election-protection contacts in a targeted Phase 2 (Week 2–3). It includes the NVRA quiet period analysis, DOJ consent decree clock, and SAVE error-rate litigation strategy in significant detail. Democracy Docket is cited 12+ times in that domain as the primary NVRA quiet period enforcement vehicle. You'll receive that document directly when Phase 2 distributes.
path_specific_additions.path_b: The framework is in active development — Democracy Docket's practitioner perspective on what the litigation tracker is missing would be the highest-value input I could receive at this stage.

gist_url_log.notes: Fill these URLs after Block 1 (Gist creation) in PHASE_1_EXECUTION_CHECKLIST.md. Use these URLs to populate {{PROPOSAL_URL}}, {{EXEC_SUMMARY_URL}}, and {{LITIGATION_TRACKER_URL}} in PHASE_1_EMAIL_TEMPLATES.md.

batch_1_success_metric.target: 2 of 5 contacts respond substantively within 7 days of first send
batch_1_success_metric.substantive_response_definition: Any reply that goes beyond 'received, thank you' — includes a question, a correction, a referral, or a request for more material
batch_1_success_metric.assessment_date_note: Check T+7 from date_sent of Contact 1 (Goodman)
batch_1_success_metric.if_below_target: Review personalization quality; check email delivery logs for bounces; consider A/B subject line variant for Batch 2
